data.body: Consideration: [Architecture] LLM relevance judgment mechanism — bash scripts cannot natively invoke an LLM. ref-library-loader.sh must clarify how the LLM relevance judgment is made. Two viable approaches: (A) ref-library-loader.sh is a bash script that prepares a filtered URL+excerpt list and writes it to stdout/a file; implementation-plan SKILL.md reads the list and performs the LLM judgment call inline before injecting citations — keeping bash as a pure data-prep utility and LLM calls in the orchestrator; (B) ref-library-loader.sh is a prompt template / agent definition rather than a bash script, dispatched by implementation-plan SKILL.md via the Agent tool. Approach A is more consistent with the existing DSO pattern (confluence-fetch.sh is bash; SKILL.md orchestrators handle LLM calls). Story E must specify the mechanism as a done definition to avoid implementation ambiguity. (taxonomy: dependency_gaps)
